question_text: Two identical containers hold the same gas at the same temperature. Container A has twice as many particles as Container B. Which statement correctly compares the pressures using the particle model?

options.label: A
options.text: Container A has the same pressure because temperature is constant

options.label: B
options.text: Container A has higher pressure because there are more particles colliding with the walls

options.label: C
options.text: Container B has higher pressure because particles have more space to move

options.label: D
options.text: Both containers have zero pressure because the particles are moving randomly

correct_answer: Container A has higher pressure because there are more particles colliding with the walls
explanation: Pressure is caused by particles colliding with container walls. Using the particle model: Container A has twice as many particles in the same volume at the same temperature. This means twice as many collisions occur per unit time and area, resulting in double the pressure. Temperature being constant means average particle speed is the same, so the difference is purely due to particle number.
